data.title: Vallourec to exhibit Delphy, their hydrogen storage solution, at Hyvolution 2026.
data.description: A recent report revealed that the global charity organization donated over $5 million to fund various projects around the world. The donation will benefit over 10,000 individuals in need of healthcare, education, and clean water. The charity aims to improve the lives of disadvantaged communities and highlights the importance of tackling poverty and inequality. The donation is part of the organization's commitment to making a positive impact on society.

data.title: Wonderful and Anthropic partner to launch Agent Builder reducing time to production by 50% and early issues by 20%.
data.description: A new Agent Builder has been introduced, boasting a reduction in time to production by as much as half and a decrease in early production issues by 20%. In an effort to assist enterprises in managing large networks of agents, Wonderful has teamed up with the frontier AI lab Anthropic. The collaboration aims to establish a strong foundation for scaling operations. The announcement was made in Amsterdam on January 23, 2026.

Looking at the medium term (1 month), forecasts suggest a range of $0.25 to $0.28. An optimistic breakout point stands at $0.19, while crucial support is identified at $0.17. These figures provide a snapshot of potential price movements for ARB in the coming weeks, indicating both target ranges and important levels to watch for.

data.title: Nasdaq seeks to eliminate position limits on Bitcoin, Ether ETF options, citing unequal treatment in derivatives markets.
data.description: A recent filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission has removed contract caps on crypto Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) options. Nasdaq has advocated for this change, stating that it aims to rectify disparities in derivatives markets. This move has significant implications for investors and traders participating in the crypto market, as it can potentially lead to increased trading volume and liquidity. By eliminating the contract caps, the potential for greater flexibility and opportunities in the crypto ETF options market is enhanced, offering a more level playing field for market participants.

data.title: OP Price Prediction: Expected to reach $0.37 by February with potential rise to $0.42; key resistance break crucial for uptrend.
data.description: Analysts are optimistic about Optimism (OP) seeing a potential increase to the $0.37-$0.42 range in the next 4 weeks, despite its current consolidation at $0.32. They have identified bullish momentum in the stock, but emphasize that a key resistance break is necessary to confirm further upside potential.

data.title: First Citizens BancShares expected to report Q4 earnings of $43.91/share, down from $45.1/share last year, with revenue of $2.29 billion, up from $2.23 billion. Company's stock value rose 1% to close at $2,203.53. Executive VP & Chief Risk Officer announced retirement.
data.description: First Citizens BancShares, Inc. (NASDAQ: FCNCA) is scheduled to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on Friday, Jan. 23 before the market opens. Analysts predict the Jacksonville, Florida-based company will post earnings of $43.91 per share for the quarter, a decrease from $45.10 per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus among analysts is that First Citizens BancShares will report quarterly revenue of $2.29 billion, up from $2.23 billion in the previous year. Lorie K. Rupp, the executive vice president and chief risk officer, announced her retirement on Jan. 14. The stock of First Citizens BancShares closed at $2,203.53 on Thursday, marking a 1% increase. More details on analyst ratings can be found on the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Compass Minerals to release Q1 fiscal 2026 results on Feb 4, 2026, with president/CEO and CFO discussing in a conference call on Feb 5, 2026, at 9:30 am ET.
data.description: Compass Minerals, a major global essential minerals provider listed on the NYSE as CMP, will announce its first-quarter fiscal 2026 results on Wednesday, February 4, 2026, after the market closes. The President and CEO, Edward C. Dowling Jr., along with CFO Peter Fjellman, will review these results during a conference call on Thursday, February 5, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. Eastern Time. Interested individuals can join the conference call through a webcast at investors.compassminerals.com or by dialing in.

data.title: Dollar General to pay $15 million for deceptive pricing, shoppers may get $10 each.
data.description: Discount retailer Dollar General Corp. (NYSE: DG) will be compensating shoppers from its 20,000 locations nationwide as part of a "deceptive" pricing class action settlement. The company has agreed to a $15 million settlement to address claims that customers were charged incorrect prices at checkout compared to the advertised shelf prices. Dollar General has not admitted to any wrongdoing in this case. The settlement covers consumers who made purchases at Dollar General stores in the US between October 10, 2016, and November 19, 2025, and were charged prices that did not align with the advertised ones. Visit Benzinga.com for the full story.

data.title: JA Solar's DeepBlue 5.0 modules are being widely deployed in utility, C&I, and residential markets for long-term value, bankability, and risk management.
data.description: As of January 23, 2026, JA Solar, a prominent player in the green energy sector, is swiftly enhancing the worldwide distribution of its DeepBlue 5.0 modules in utility, commercial & industrial, and residential sectors. This expansion comes as developers show a growing interest in long-lasting value, bankability, and risk mitigation strategies.
